subject: english
topic_title: Requirements of the Examination on Orals III
learning_objectives: Explain Requirements of the Examination on Orals III
Identify key ideas in Requirements of the Examination on Orals III
Apply Requirements of the Examination on Orals III concepts in examples

sections.heading: Content
sections.content: The aspects of oral English tested in the examination include the following: vowels, consonants, rhymes, word-stress, emphatic stress and phonetic symbols.

sections.heading: Vowels
sections.content: There are twenty vowels in the English language. They include twelve pure vowels and eight diphthongs.

sections.heading: Consonants
sections.content: There are twenty-four consonants in the English language. They are classified into three groups: place of articulation, manner of articulation and state of the glottis.

sections.heading: Word Stress
sections.content: This refers to the amount of pressure exerted when pronouncing a particular syllable of a word. The syllable with the stress is marked with a slash at the top in the dictionary.

sections.heading: Emphatic Stress
sections.content:
This is the stressing of particular word more than other words in a sentence. Such a stress normally has its implications in terms of the meaning of the sentence.

Rhymes: When two or more words have a similar sound at the end, they are said to rhyme.
